clean.verdict_disclaimer: This report presents observed mechanisms and their validity tiers. It does NOT self-validate a moral or malicious verdict. The scanner makes the invisible visible; the reader performs the recognition.

tier_legend.2: Structural — pattern of concealment, context may justify

findings.description: 1 content-stream region(s) contain zero-width character(s). These are not rendered but are preserved through conforming text extraction.
1 content-stream region(s) contain bidirectional control character(s). These can reorder displayed text without changing its underlying byte order.

1 content-stream region(s) contain Unicode TAG character(s) (U+E0000-U+E007F). TAG characters do not render but carry ASCII payloads preserved through text extraction — a known prompt-injection vector.

1 word(s) contain characters from non-Latin Unicode blocks that visually impersonate Latin letters (Cyrillic/Greek/Armenian/Cherokee/fullwidth/mathematical). Common in phishing spoofs and prompt-injection label swaps.
Text rendered with render mode 3 (invisible). 38 character(s) of hidden text extracted.

inversion_recovery.concealed: INVISIBLE PAYLOAD: meet at clock tower

findings.description: Font size 0.300pt is below human-readable threshold (1.0pt).
Text color RGB(1.00, 1.00, 1.00) matches page background luminance (delta 0.000), and no coloured fill is drawn behind the span.
Two text spans share 98% of their bounding-box area but contain different text. The later-drawn span occludes the other visually; both survive in the text layer.
Document contains 2 %%EOF markers, indicating 1 incremental update(s). Prior revisions may contain content not rendered in the current view.
Document declares /OpenAction — action triggered on document open. Action subtype: /JavaScript.
Document declares /AA — additional actions triggered by document events (open, close, save, print, etc.).
Document declares a /JavaScript name tree in the catalog. JavaScript can execute on open, on user action, or in response to form events.
1 Optional Content Group(s) hidden by default. Content present in file but not rendered in default view.

Font /Fadv on page 1 carries a ToUnicode CMap with 2 entr(y/ies) that map visible glyph CIDs to adversarial Unicode targets (zero-width, bidi control, TAG, or Latin homoglyph). Visible text and extracted text will diverge.

inversion_recovery.concealed: CID<65> → 'е'  [homoglyph U+0435 (looks like 'e')]; CID<6F> → 'о'  [homoglyph U+043E (looks like 'o')]
